facts.text: Norwich was formally established on January 19, 1793, carved out of the historic Town of Jericho and the Town of Union.
The city’s name comes from Norwich, England, reflecting the settlers’ desire to bring a piece of home to upstate New York.
Condensed milk pioneer Gail Borden founded his company in Norwich, turning the town into the birthplace of a dairy innovation that changed America.
From 1845 until 1961, the Maydole Hammer Factory operated in Norwich, earning international recognition for its iron products.
Norwich’s transportation history includes the Chenango Canal, the New York, Ontario and Western Railway, and the Delaware, Lackawanna and Western Railroad.
The city hosts the Northeast Classic Car Museum, which houses over 160 classic cars, including the world’s largest collection of Franklin automobiles.
